rationale: Ground truth shows only a maintainer tagging two colleagues for review ('PTAL') — the issue was still open/under triage, not fixed or closed. Both candidates instead fabricate a confident, detailed 'already merged and closed' narrative with specific unverifiable file/line citations, so both score low on correctness for contradicting the actual (uncertain) thread state. X is the better of the two: it reads like an actual maintainer reply, explicitly engages with and explains why the reporter's two suggested fixes were not chosen, and gives a coherent narrative. Y is weaker in execution — it leaks raw internal tool scaffolding ('Run report', a literal task dict, a redundant 'Linked issues/PRs' section) that would look like a broken artifact rather than a real GitHub comment, hurting its usefulness despite similar fabricated technical content.
